title: Core Concepts of Algorithmic Game Theory
description: A conceptual diagram illustrating the intersection of computer science and economic theory, focusing on the analysis of strategic interactions in computational settings.
elements: Solution Concepts (Nash Equilibrium, Price of Anarchy)
relationships: Computer Science provides tools to analyze and design efficient systems
Economics provides models of rational strategic behavior
Strategic Agents interact within Computational Systems
Solution Concepts are used to predict and evaluate outcomes
